Albert Boxler Sommerberg Riesling L

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Domaine Albert BoxlerAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Boxler's L parcel selection on Sommerberg (formerly called Jeunes Vignes), younger granite vines but the same incisive mineral character.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing

Domaine Dirler-CadeAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Dirler-Cade's Saering Riesling from sandstone slopes around Guebwiller. Biodynamic and bone-dry; the maison's everyday Grand Cru.

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Famille HugelAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Estate-only Riesling from the family's holdings in Riquewihr and Zellenberg, bottled dry and unoaked. Hugel's mid-tier Riesling.

Jean Becker Froehn Muscat

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Fruity

Domaine Jean BeckerAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Jean Becker's biodynamic Muscat from Grand Cru Froehn near Zellenberg. Bone-dry but with the Muscat aromatic lift; among the few standalone Alsace Grand Cru Muscat bottlings.

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing

Domaine JosmeyerAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Josmeyer's Le Kottabe is the maison's signature dry Riesling, named for an antique Greek wine-drinking game. Bone dry, citrus and stone.

Kuentz-BasAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Kuentz-Bas's Trois Chateaux Riesling, named for the three medieval castles above Husseren-les-Chateaux. The maison's village-level dry Riesling.

Leon BeyerAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Beyer's mid-tier dry Riesling from Eguisheim plots, more accessible in price than the Comtes but in the same austere mineral idiom.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing

Domaine OstertagAlsace€€€€€€€€Still White

Andre Ostertag's Heissenberg Riesling from sandstone slopes near Nothalten, alongside Muenchberg. Pure, dry, biodynamic; the maison's everyday Bas-Rhin Riesling.

Domaines SchlumbergerAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Schlumberger's most approachable Grand Cru Riesling, from sandstone slopes lower than Kitterle. Brisk citrus and stone.

Maison TrimbachAlsace€€€€€€€Still White

Trimbach's house-style Riesling, blending fruit from multiple villages around Ribeauville. Bone-dry, citrus and stone, the textbook introduction to Alsace Riesling.

Terres Dorées' Chardonnay from the golden limestone of Pierres Dorées: between Mâcon and Chablis in character, with peach-blossom freshness, chalky mineral and restrained richness.

Pierre-Marie Chermette's estate Beaujolais AOC from the Vissoux's Pierres Dorées plots: whole-cluster, unforced, clean red-cherry Gamay made in the traditional pre-industrial Beaujolais style.

P'tit Caillou ('little stone') is Vionnet's entry-level Beaujolais AOC: unpretentious, fresh Gamay with crunchy red fruit and mineral granite lift for immediate drinking.

★★★★★★★★★★

Maison Georges DuboeufBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Duboeuf's Régnié from the youngest cru: the typical light, fragrant and raspberry-driven character of the appellation, assembled from selected growers in Régnié-Durette.

Saint-Amour

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Easygoing · Refreshing

Domaine de la CigaloiseBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red
Cuvée page →

Aile d'Argent

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Mouton RothschildBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still White

White wine of Mouton Rothschild from 7 hectares of sandy-gravelly soils, reviving a historic Medoc tradition. Rich and complex; Sauvignon Blanc provides drive while Semillon adds texture; partial barrel fermentation contributes subtle vanilla without overwhelming fruit.

Cuvée page →

Bauduc Les Trois Hectares Blanc

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still White

Single-plot Semillon from 75-year-old vines in a three-hectare block; the house premium white. Dry and elegant with the waxy, textured Semillon character that recalls white Pessac-Leognan at a fraction of the price. Anne Krebiehl MW-praised.

Cuvée page →

Blanc de Lynch-Bages

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still White

Historically one of the first white wines produced by a Grand Cru Classe in the Medoc. Primarily Sauvignon Blanc driven with Semillon body; partially barrel-fermented but retains freshness through tank-vinified component.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Bauduc Sauvignon Blanc

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still White

Bauduc's benchmark white and most popular wine: 100% Sauvignon Blanc, stainless steel fermented for maximum freshness and varietal definition. Crisp, dry, and food-versatile; house white at Rick Stein's fish restaurants for over a decade.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Pape Clement Blanc

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still White

A classified Graves white from one of Bordeaux's most historic estates. Sauvignon Blanc leads with fresh pink grapefruit and lime blossom; Semillon adds body and Sauvignon Gris a distinctive floral note. 100% new oak fermentation produces a rich, mineral, long-lived dry white.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Smith Haut Lafitte Blanc

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still White

A consistently top-rated Pessac-Leognan white dominated by Sauvignon Blanc with distinctive Sauvignon Gris character. Honeyed grapefruit, crushed stone, and elderflower on the nose; vibrant citrus and mineral freshness on the palate. Frequently earns 95-98 points in recent vintages.

Cuvée page →

Cos d'Estournel Blanc

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Cos d'EstournelBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still White

One of the most prestigious white Bordeaux wines from Saint-Estephe, produced from a 10-hectare dedicated white wine parcel. Fresh, vibrant, and barrel-fermented; the Sauvignon Blanc drive and Semillon texture achieve remarkable complexity.

Cuvée page →

Le Blanc de Chateau de la Riviere

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

An unusual Bordeaux Blanc built on Sauvignon Gris rather than Semillon, giving tropical and citrus character with a rich, textured palate. Only 5,600 bottles produced annually; fermented mostly in stainless steel to preserve freshness.

Cuvée page →

Le Lys de Lafaurie-Peyraguey

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau Lafaurie-PeyragueyBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

A dry white Bordeaux introduced in 2014 from the Lafaurie-Peyraguey estate in Bommes. Sauvignon Blanc-dominant with Semillon adding body; white flowers, lemon blossom, and fresh citrus lead a mineral, herb-tinged palate. An accessible entry into the Sauternes estate's terroir as a dry expression.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Cheval Blanc

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Chateau Cheval BlancBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still White

Barrel-fermented Sauvignon Blanc and Semillon blend from Cheval Blanc's estate, classified as Bordeaux Blanc since Saint-Emilion AOC prohibits white varieties. Elegant citrus and flint profile with saline length.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Haut Lafitte Blanc

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still White

The second white wine of Smith Haut Lafitte. Sauvignon Blanc-driven with Semillon adding roundness; lime tree, verbena, and citrus oil aromas lead a fresh, floral palate with elderflower and white peach. Vibrant acidity makes it an excellent aperitif and shellfish partner.

Cuvée page →

Pavillon Blanc du Château Margaux

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Château MargauxBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

A rare Sauvignon Blanc white Bordeaux of extraordinary mineral precision: gunsmoke, slate and saline drive a taut citrus frame that ages beautifully over a decade or more.

Cuvée page →

R de Rieussec

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau RieussecBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

The dry white wine of Chateau Rieussec, a relative newcomer first produced in 1999. A fresh, citrussy Sauvignon Blanc and Semillon blend from the Fargues-de-Langon terroir with herbal and mineral freshness; lighter-styled than the Sauternes grand vin but sharing its Semillon richness.

Cuvée page →

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing

Domaine Coche-DuryBurgundy€€€€€€€€€Still White

A cult Aligote far beyond its appellation, taut and saline with citrus, herbs and a stony finish.

Cuvée page →

Bourgogne Blanc Les Petits Musigny

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Full-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing

Domaine Comte Georges de VogueBurgundy€€€€€€€€€€Still White

A rare white from young Musigny vines, declassified to Bourgogne, with citrus, white flowers and a saline depth.

A simple, fresh regional Chardonnay with citrus, orchard fruit and a clean finish.

A dry Grosses Gewachs from old vines in the Trittenheimer Apotheke: precise, taut and finely mineral from steep slate.

Adenzia Bianco blends Grillo and Inzolia from Baglio del Cristo's chalky Agrigento limestone soils: the combination yields a fresh, almond-citrus white with the mineral depth that characterises this distinctive terroir.

Caravaglio's Salina Bianco is a dry expression of Malvasia di Lipari: the grape's characteristic floral and apricot aromatics expressed without sweetness, blended with Inzolia for freshness. A charming, mineral everyday white from the Aeolian Islands.

CusumanoSicily€€€€€€Still White

Donnafugata Anthilia

★★★★★★★★★★

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€Still White

Anthilia is Donnafugata's flagship entry white: Catarratto and Grecanico from the Contessa Entellina estate in a fresh, floral and mineral style that has defined quality Sicilian white wine for three decades.

Donnafugata PantelleriaSicily€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

Kabir is Donnafugata's lighter Pantelleria sweet wine: fresher and less concentrated than Ben Rye, from Zibibbo grapes dried for a shorter period, producing a floral, juicy dessert wine of great charm.

Donnafugata Lighea

Lighea is Donnafugata's dry-to-off-dry Zibibbo table wine from Pantelleria: fresh, aromatic, with the grape's characteristic muscat-like floral and apricot character in a lighter, immediately approachable style.

Donnafugata Lighea

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Fruity

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€Still White

Lighea blends Zibibbo's heady Muscat-like perfume with native white grapes in a delicately off-dry aromatic style: floral, peachy, with balancing acidity that keeps it from cloying.

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Feudi del PisciottoSicily€€€€€€Still White

Feudi del Pisciotto's varietal Grillo from the Val di Noto estate delivers the grape's characteristic citrus-blossom and sea-mineral freshness from a warm inland site moderated by its position on the edge of the Sughereta reserve.

Cuvée page →

Feudo Montoni Grillo della Timpa

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Feudo MontoniSicily€€€€€€Still White

Grillo grown at altitude in central Sicily develops an unusual aromatic tension; this steel-only white is crisper and more mineral than coastal Grillos with notable herbal lift.

Cuvée page →

Firriato Cavanera Etna Bianco

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Firriato Cavanera EtneaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Cavanera's Etna Bianco provides an accessible entry into north-slope Carricante from the Firriato Etna estate: fresh, mineral and cleanly made.

Cuvée page →

Firriato Chiaramonte Bianco

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

FirriatoSicily€€€€€€Still White

Firriato's entry white blends the native almond-citrus character of Catarratto with Chardonnay's stone-fruit roundness in a clean, everyday Sicilian white from the Trapani estate.

Cuvée page →

Frank Cornelissen Susucaru

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Funky

Frank CornelissenSicily€€€€€€€Still Rose

Susucaru is Cornelissen's zero-addition rosato: brief skin contact on Nerello Mascalese yields a pale, mineral-driven rose with wild red fruit and Etna's characteristic volcanic salinity.

Cuvée page →

Girolamo Russo Etna Bianco

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Girolamo RussoSicily€€€€€€€Still White

A refreshing, organically farmed Etna Bianco from Russo's north-slope holdings: Carricante-led with bright citrus, saline mineral and the clean volcanic freshness of the estate.

Cuvée page →

Graci Etna Bianco

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

GraciSicily€€€€€€€Still White

A bright, citrus-driven Etna Bianco blending Carricante and Catarratto: saline, mineral, refreshing - capturing the north slope's volcanic energy in a fresh and approachable white.

Cuvée page →

Hauner Malvasia delle Lipari Naturale

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Hauner (Malvasia delle Lipari)Sicily€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

The Naturale style is a lighter, fresher take on Malvasia delle Lipari: less concentrated than the Passito but showing the grape's characteristic floral-apricot aromatics and the volcanic salinity of the Aeolian Islands in a more immediately approachable register.

Cuvée page →

Hauner Salina Rosso

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Hauner (Malvasia delle Lipari)Sicily€€€€€€Still Red

Hauner's Salina Rosso is a rare Aeolian red from Nero d'Avola and the indigenous Corinto Nero: light-bodied, herb-scented and saline-mineral, reflecting the island's volcanic coastal terroir in a red wine format.

Cuvée page →

Mandrarossa Catarratto

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Settesoli MandrarossaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Mandrarossa's varietal Catarratto is the most characteristically Sicilian of the range: Sicily's most widely planted white variety in its simplest, freshest expression from Menfi's coastal estate.

Cuvée page →

Mandrarossa Grillo

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Settesoli MandrarossaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Mandrarossa's coastal Grillo from Menfi shows the grape's Mediterranean character: sun-warmed citrus blossom and peach with sea-salt mineral and high acidity from close proximity to the Tyrrhenian coast.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Grappoli del Grillo

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Marco De BartoliSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Grappoli del Grillo is De Bartoli's dry still table wine from Grillo, made without oxidation: a fresh, mineral white that showcases the grape's natural salinity, citrus character and almond-bitter finish when vinified as a conventional still wine rather than a perpetuo.

Cuvée page →

Murgo Etna Bianco

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

MurgoSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Murgo's Etna Bianco is a bright, fresh expression of eastern-slope Carricante and Catarratto: floral, citrus-forward and mineral-clean.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti Il Frappato

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Occhipinti's varietal Frappato is perhaps the finest expression of the variety: achingly delicate, chalk-mineral, fresh raspberry and rose-hip with extraordinary tension for its light weight.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti SP68 Bianco

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still White

The white counterpart to SP68 Rosso: Grecanico and Zibibbo from volcanic soils, aromatically intense, mineral and saline with a citrus-peach freshness.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti SP68 Rosso

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Funky

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Named after the SP68 road through Vittoria, Occhipinti's signature blend is a Frappato-dominant light red of extraordinary mineral lift, wild-berry purity and racy acidity.

Cuvée page →

Palmento Costanzo Mofete Etna Bianco

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Palmento CostanzoSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Mofete Bianco is the Palmento Costanzo estate white: bright and mineral-driven from organically farmed Carricante on Etna's north slope in Contrada Santo Spirito.

Cuvée page →

Pellegrino Moscato di Pantelleria

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Cantine PellegrinoSicily€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

The Moscato di Pantelleria is a lighter, fresher sweet wine style compared to the passito: Zibibbo grapes not fully dried produce a wine of floral freshness, bright apricot character and lower alcohol, ideal as an aperitivo or light dessert wine.

Cuvée page →

Pietradolce Etna Bianco

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€Still White

A fresh, immediately drinkable Etna Bianco: citrus-forward with Carricante's characteristic volcanic salinity and clean mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Frappato

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Planeta Dorilli (Vittoria)Sicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Planeta's Frappato from Vittoria is an accessible, fresh expression of the variety: raspberry and rose aromas, light body and high acidity make it one of Sicily's most versatile lighter reds.

Planeta Grillo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's varietal Grillo from the Menfi estate shows the Mediterranean coastal character of the grape: citrus verbena, sea-salt mineral and herbal freshness with the clean precision of Planeta's winemaking.

Planeta La Segreta Bianco

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€Still White

La Segreta Bianco blends native Grecanico with aromatic international varieties in Planeta's most accessible white: fresh, aromatic and immediately appealing with stone-fruit and citrus freshness.

Planeta Sciaranuova Carricante

★★★★★★★★★★

Planeta Sciaranuova (Etna)Sicily€€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's Etna Carricante from the Sciaranuova estate: the volcanic freshness of the north slope expressed through Planeta's clean, precise winemaking style.

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Leone d'Almerita is the everyday entry point of the Tasca range: Catarratto and Grillo in a fresh, clean aromatic white for versatile food matching.

★★★★★★★★★★

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Created for the fiftieth anniversary of Lucio and Franca Tasca's wedding, Nozze d'Oro pairs Inzolia's stone-fruit softness with Sauvignon Blanc's aromatic herbal freshness in an enduring premium Sicilian white.

Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Bianco

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Sicily's most widely distributed native-white blend: Regaleali Bianco is a reliable, aromatic white of citrus and stone fruit freshness from the island's historic Tasca estate.

Erse is Fessina's entry-level Etna Bianco: bright, mineral-driven Carricante from the organically farmed Rovittello estate at 670 metres, with the clean saline freshness of north-slope white Etna.

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

AdamiVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Bosco di Gica (Gica's wood) is a mid-range Brut from Adami, positioned between Dei Casel and the Rive Giardino. The Brut style emphasises the Valdobbiadene zone's fresh, mineral character without the Ex Dry sweetness.

Cuvée page →

Adami dei Casel Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

AdamiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The entry-level Extra Dry from Adami. Dei Casel (meaning 'of the hamlet') has been the house's everyday accessible label since the early decades of production. Consistent, clean and approachable.

Cuvée page →

Bele Casel Asolo Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bele CaselVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Asolo Prosecco DOCG is produced on the south-facing slopes of the Asolo hills, southwest of Valdobbiadene. Bele Casel uses indigenous Perera and Verdiso alongside Glera for a more complex aromatic profile than monovarietal Prosecco.

Cuvée page →

Bele Casel Costalunga Asolo Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Bele CaselVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Costalunga is Bele Casel's single-vineyard Brut from the Costalunga slope within the Asolo DOCG zone. The steep south-facing aspect and altitude produce a more concentrated and mineral brut than the entry-level Extra Dry.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Crede Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The flagship multi-varietal Valdobbiadene Brut from Bisol. 'Crede' refers to the clay-limestone (crete) soils of the Valdobbiadene hills. Inclusion of indigenous Verdiso, Perera and Bianchetta adds aromatic complexity absent in single-varietal Prosecco.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Jeio Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Jeio is the accessible everyday Brut tier from Bisol 1542, positioned below the premium Crede multi-varietal and the Rive expressions. The Glera-dominant blend with minor Verdiso and Bianchetta adds a touch of aromatic complexity to a clean, food-friendly Brut.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Relio Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Extra Dry in Prosecco parlance is slightly sweeter (12-17 g/l residual sugar) than Brut. Relio is a soft, fruit-forward style with rounded acidity, suited to lighter food pairings and aperitivo moments.

Cuvée page →

Bisol Rive di Campea Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Bisol 1542Veneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The Rive di Campea sits at higher elevation than most Valdobbiadene Rive sites. The altitude emphasises acidity and mineral character, producing one of the zone's most vertically inclined expressions.

Cuvée page →

Bortolomiol Senior Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

BortolomiolVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The flagship Extra Dry from Bortolomiol, founded in 1949 by Giuliano Bortolomiol. Named 'Senior' in tribute to the founder, this is a classic off-dry Valdobbiadene DOCG with the balance of sweetness and freshness that made the Extra Dry category popular.

Cuvée page →

Ca' La Bionda Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Ca' La BiondaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Ca' La Bionda's Classico Superiore reflects the upper Marano valley's mineral character in a lighter-bodied, tart cherry register; crisp, food-friendly and genuinely typical of the traditional Valpolicella style.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Soave Rocca Sveva Soave Classico

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Cantina di SoaveVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The cooperative's entry-level Soave Classico DOC provides excellent access to the appellation's almond-blossom Garganega character at very accessible price; clean, fresh and food-versatile.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Soave Rocca Sveva Soave Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Cantina di SoaveVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Rocca Sveva is the flagship quality expression from the Cantina di Soave cooperative; a consistent, well-made Garganega-dominant Superiore DOCG that provides excellent value access to the appellation's almond-mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Case Paolin Asolo Prosecco Superiore Extra Brut

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Case PaolinVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Case Paolin's signature expression from the Montello foothills in the Asolo DOCG zone. The Giusto family uses indigenous varieties including Verdiso and Perera for complexity, and favours a lower dosage Extra Brut style for a drier, mineral-driven Asolo Prosecco Superiore.

Cuvée page →

Cavalchina Bardolino Chiaretto Classico

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

CavalchinaVeneto€€€€€€Still Rose

Cavalchina's Chiaretto Classico extends the estate's Lake Garda portfolio from white to rosato. Brief skin contact on Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara produces a pale copper-pink wine with fresh berry and rose aromatics; dry and food-versatile.

Cuvée page →

Cavalchina Bianco di Custoza

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

CavalchinaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The Piona family's benchmark Bianco di Custoza. The DOC requires a minimum of five grape varieties, and Cavalchina's multi-varietal blend from morainic glacial soils south of Lake Garda delivers a crisp, food-friendly white with genuine complexity from the variety mix.

Cuvée page →

Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Col Vetoraz SpumantiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The entry Brut Valdobbiadene DOCG from Col Vetoraz. The estate's altitude above Santo Stefano produces a distinctly cool-climate Glera with sharper acidity and finer aromatics than lower-valley peers.

Cuvée page →

Corte Gardoni Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Corte GardoniVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The Piccoli family's Bianco di Custoza from the morainic rim at Valeggio sul Mincio. The glacially deposited soils on the southern slope of the Lake Garda moraine produce a fresh, food-friendly white with characteristic saline mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Fasoli Gino Soave Classico Mael

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Fasoli GinoVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Mael is the flagship Soave Classico from Fasoli Gino, one of the Soave zone's pioneering certified organic estates. ICEA-certified organic viticulture at Colognola ai Colli on volcanic basalt soils gives this Garganega a particularly expressive mineral and orchard-fruit profile.

Cuvée page →

Foss Marai Drusian Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Foss MaraiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Drusian is the flagship Extra Dry from Foss Marai, named after the locality of the Guia vineyards. Softer and more aromatic than the Brut, with white peach and acacia blossom in an off-dry, easy-drinking style.

Cuvée page →

Foss Marai Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Foss MaraiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The house Brut from Foss Marai at Guia di Valdobbiadene. The Guia area has some of the steepest terraced vineyards in the DOCG zone; the Brut reflects this with a crisp, mineral precision.

Cuvée page →

Gini Soave Classico

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

GiniVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Gini's estate Soave Classico 100% Garganega captures the family's 17th-century vine heritage in a fresh, mineral, everyday expression; the racy acidity and almond-blossom purity of the volcanic Monteforte basalt terroir at an accessible price.

Cuvée page →

Gini Soave Classico La Frosca

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

GiniVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

La Frosca from the Gini family's oldest Garganega vines on the highest basalt-volcanic slopes of Monteforte d'Alpone; the chalky mineral texture, fennel herb and white-peach fruit represent the Classico zone's basalt terroir in its purest expression.

Cuvée page →

Gorgo Bardolino Classico DOC

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

GorgoVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Gorgo's Bardolino Classico from vineyards along the eastern Garda shore. A light, fresh Corvina-Rondinella red for everyday drinking, complementing the estate's main Custoza white wine focus.

Cuvée page →

Gorgo Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

GorgoVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The Gorgo estate at Custoza di Valeggio sul Mincio produces one of the most consistently mineral Bianco di Custoza expressions. The morainic glacial soils on the southern slope of the Lake Garda amphitheatre impart a saline mineral quality to the Garganega-Trebbiano-Cortese blend.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Tacchetto Bardolino Chiaretto Classico

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Romantic

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€Still Rose

Tacchetto is a single-vineyard Bardolino Chiaretto from the estate's oldest Corvina vineyard. Chiaretto (meaning 'light' or 'pale') is the rosato of Garda, made by short skin contact on Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara. Pale salmon-pink with delicate berry and rose aromatics.

Cuvée page →

Inama Oratorio di San Lorenzo Soave Superiore DOCG

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Inama's Oratorio di San Lorenzo is the estate's second single-vineyard Soave Superiore from the adjacent San Lorenzo parcel, showing the same volcanic basalt minerality as Foscarino but with a slightly more herbal, fennel-accented character.

Cuvée page →

Inama Soave Classico Superiore Vigneti di Foscarino

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Vigneti di Foscarino is Inama's pure basalt-volcanic Soave Classico from the historic Foscarino hill, the most volcanically active terroir in the Classico zone; 100% Garganega barrel-fermented for a mineral, almond-hazelnut complexity of striking length.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Il Tralcetto Pinot Grigio delle Venezie

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still White

The volume accessible white from Italo Cescon. Pinot Grigio delle Venezie DOC is the broad appellation for Pinot Grigio from the Tre Venezie; the Cescon version is produced from Piave valley floor vineyards and represents the commercial, food-versatile tier of the estate alongside the character-driven Raboso.

Cuvée page →

Maculan Pino and Toi Breganze Bianco

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

MaculanVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pino and Toi (Pine and You in Veneto dialect) is Maculan's accessible dry white, a refreshing blend of the two Pinots and Tocai for everyday drinking. It represents the lighter, approachable side of the Breganze DOC in contrast to the estate's intense dessert wines.

Cuvée page →

Monte Tondo Soave Classico DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Monte TondoVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Monte Tondo's entry-level Soave Classico DOC from vineyards within the historic Classico zone. A clean, accessible Garganega-Trebbiano blend for everyday drinking; lighter than the Casette Foscarin Superiore.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Rive di Pergola Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Racy acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The Rive designation identifies a single-municipality harvest on steep hillsides. Di Pergola offers stony minerality and tighter acidity than the multi-commune Rustico, reflecting the specific clay-limestone terroir.

Cuvée page →

Nino Franco Rustico Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Nino FrancoVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The benchmark entry-level Prosecco Superiore DOCG from Nino Franco. Pale straw with fine persistent perlage; white stone fruit and floral notes lead to a crisp, dry finish.

Cuvée page →

Ottella Lugana DOC

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

OttellaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Ottella's standard Lugana DOC is produced from Turbiana (the local name for Verdicchio in the Lugana DOC zone) from estate vineyards at Peschiera del Garda on the southeastern shore of Lake Garda. The clay-limestone soils of the Lugana plain give Turbiana its distinctive almond mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pieropan's estate Soave Classico is the reference-standard expression of the appellation: Garganega's characteristic almond-blossom and white-peach aromas with a saline, mineral finish from the volcanic basalt hillside soils of the Classico zone.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico Calvarino

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Calvarino is Pieropan's basalt and limestone single-vineyard Soave, planted in 1924 to Garganega and Trebbiano di Soave; the volcanic basalt soils give a crackling acidity and mineral depth far beyond the appellation average, with exceptional ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Pra Monte Grande Soave Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

PraVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Pra's Monte Grande from the basalt-rich Monteforte hillside is a benchmark organic Soave Classico: Graziano Pra's organic viticulture and extended lees contact produce a pure, mineral, textural wine of genuine complexity and age-worthiness for the price.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi San Vincenzo

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€Still White

San Vincenzo is Anselmi's most widely distributed wine, a Garganega-Chardonnay Veneto IGT that captures the almond-blossom freshness of the Soave zone in a light, food-friendly, affordable white. The Chardonnay adds body without altering the Garganega character.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Giustino B Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

The historical benchmark of Ruggeri, first produced when the house was founded in 1950. Giustino B is named after Giustino Bisol, who co-founded the house; this Extra Dry expression became one of the most recognised Prosecco Superiore labels in the zone.

Cuvée page →

Ruggeri Quartese Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Ruggeri and C.Veneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Ruggeri's brut style for occasions calling for a leaner, drier Prosecco Superiore than the classic Extra Dry. Quartese is named after a Valdobbiadene locality and emphasises the zone's mineral freshness.

Cuvée page →

Suavia Soave Classico

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

SuaviaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Suavia's estate Soave Classico is a fresh, mineral, everyday expression of Garganega from the village of Soave; classic almond-blossom aromatics with a crisp, saline finish perfect for aperitivo or seafood.

Cuvée page →

Suavia Soave Classico Monte Carbonare

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

SuaviaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Suavia's Monte Carbonare from the village of Soave itself: 100% Garganega from a steep volcanic tufa hillside with century-old vines; lees-aged in large casks producing exceptional mineral depth, citrus-almond precision and great cellaring potential.

Cuvée page →

Vignalta Pinot Bianco Colli Euganei

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

VignaltaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Pinot Bianco on the volcanic soils of the Euganean Hills produces a wine of notable mineral freshness. The volcanic basalt structure of the Colli Euganei translates into a flinty mineral quality in white wines rarely seen in the Veneto plains.

Cuvée page →

Villa Sandi Il Fresco Prosecco DOC Treviso Brut

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Villa SandiVeneto€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Villa Sandi's volume Prosecco DOC expression. Il Fresco is the globally distributed everyday label from the Palladian villa estate in Crocetta del Montello; consistent, approachable and affordable at the DOC level.

Cuvée page →

Villa Sandi Opere Trevigiane Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Light-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Villa SandiVeneto€€€€€€€Sparkling Tank

Opere Trevigiane is Villa Sandi's premium DOCG Brut, positioned between Il Fresco DOC and the Cartizze grand cru. It uses Glera from selected hillside parcels within the Valdobbiadene DOCG zone for a more structured brut expression.

Cuvée page →

Viviani Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Refreshing

VivianiVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Viviani's Classico Superiore from the high Mazzano ridge shows the altitude freshness that characterises all the estate's wines: iron mineral, tart cherry and mountain herbs in a lean, food-loving medium-red.
